title: Heresay Homicide — Level 5: Senior Detective Case
venue: Heresay Speakeasy at Circa 1857 Antique Store, Baton Rouge
description: Level 5: Senior Detective Case for Heresay Homicide. Focus: layered lies and cover-up.

answerKey.method: Used a duplicate Circa 1857 key to move Elise’s emergency medication and frame Calvin with a staged voicemail
answerKey.motive: To cover a long-running appraisal fraud scheme that Elise finally traced back to her
answerKey.keyEvidence: duplicate key impression, staged voicemail metadata, and appraisal fraud binder
answerKey.explanation: Vivian LaSalle is exposed when rumors are separated from verified proof. The shared Heresay/Circa passage explains opportunity, duplicate key impression, staged voicemail metadata, and appraisal fraud binder proves the cover-up, and the motive was to cover a long-running appraisal fraud scheme that Elise finally traced back to her.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Heresay Homicide begins at Heresay Speakeasy at Circa 1857 Antique Store, Baton Rouge. This session is Level 5: Senior Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Venue Note
publicClues.text: The Heresay Speakeasy shares a wall, service passage, storage, and antique displays with Circa 1857. Movement between the bar and antique store matters.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Review evidence, not gossip, before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Review evidence, not gossip, before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Review evidence, not gossip, before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Review evidence, not gossip, before answering.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Elise missed a call from Vivian LaSalle minutes before the staff passage door opened.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A recovered audio fragment captures someone saying, “Don’t repeat what you heard in the antique room.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Elise texted a friend: “The rumor is wrong. I found the paperwork inside Circa.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: A deleted reply from Vivian says, “You should have left hearsay alone.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Location pings place Elise between the speakeasy hallway and the Circa 1857 back room.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Vivian’s device appears near the shared passage during the evidence gap.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A payment tied to the antique-store event account was edited the same afternoon.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The transaction memo matches the motive trail: to cover a long-running appraisal fraud scheme that Elise finally traced back to her.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo captures the antique display case behind Elise before the crowd moves.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Zooming the reflection shows Vivian near the service passage, not at the place witnesses claimed.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A rumor post claims a stranger did it, but the upload time is after the hallway camera gap.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: An edited caption removes a line about Elise “checking the Circa ledger.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Elise saved Vivian LaSalle with a note: “ask about Circa discrepancy.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: A staff contact card lists who had keys to the shared antique-store passage.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: hearsay points everywhere, but only confirmed access narrows the suspect list.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Elise’s note reads: “If I disappear, check the antique inventory first.”

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: File export shows the key proof: duplicate key impression, staged voicemail metadata, and appraisal fraud binder.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: Metadata confirms the incriminating file was opened before the public argument.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: Elise searched “how to verify antique appraisal fraud” before arriving.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Someone searched “how long before deleted voicemail metadata updates” after the collapse.

rounds.title: Round 1: The Rumor Starts
rounds.objective: Separate the first rumor from what the scene actually proves.
rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. Focus on the rumor starts. In this case, rumors are not proof until evidence confirms them.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Decide what the evidence proves before the next rumor spreads.

rounds.objective: Compare movement between Heresay Speakeasy and the attached Circa 1857 antique store.
rounds.dialogue: Round 2 is open. Focus on the antique store passage. In this case, rumors are not proof until evidence confirms them.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 2 is nearly complete. Decide what the evidence proves before the next rumor spreads.

rounds.title: Round 3: The Statement Breaks
rounds.objective: Find which witness account cannot survive the digital evidence.
rounds.dialogue: Round 3 is open. Focus on the statement breaks. In this case, rumors are not proof until evidence confirms them.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 3 is nearly complete. Decide what the evidence proves before the next rumor spreads.

rounds.title: Round 4: The Motive Behind the Whisper
rounds.objective: Connect the money, reputation, or fraud motive to the suspect.
rounds.dialogue: Round 4 is open. Focus on the motive behind the whisper. In this case, rumors are not proof until evidence confirms them.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 4 is nearly complete. Decide what the evidence proves before the next rumor spreads.

rounds.title: Round 5: The Truth Under the Hearsay
rounds.objective: Use the final physical clue to name the killer and method.
rounds.dialogue: Round 5 is open. Focus on the truth under the hearsay. In this case, rumors are not proof until evidence confirms them.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 5 is nearly complete. Decide what the evidence proves before the next rumor spreads.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Heresay Homicide. You are playing Level 5: Senior Detective Case. Heresay Speakeasy is attached to Circa 1857 antique store, so every rumor must be tested against movement, access, and physical evidence.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 5: Senior Detective Case. The culprit was Vivian LaSalle. The method: used a duplicate Circa 1857 key to move Elise’s emergency medication and frame Calvin with a staged voicemail. The motive: to cover a long-running appraisal fraud scheme that Elise finally traced back to her. The proof: duplicate key impression, staged voicemail metadata, and appraisal fraud binder.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What should detectives treat as evidence instead of hearsay?

options.label: A guest saying they heard a stranger arguing
A reposted rumor with no timestamp
A bartender repeating what another patron said
The confirmed scene detail that matches the physical evidence

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Why does the Circa 1857 connection matter?

options.label: The antique store proves the victim was shopping
The building has old furniture
The crowd liked the vintage decor
The shared passage lets someone move between the speakeasy and antique store unseen

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which clue breaks the witness rumor?

options.label: A rumor that the lights flickered
A receipt for ordinary drinks
A blurry photo of the crowd
The timestamp/reflection places Vivian near the passage
Jealousy over karaoke seating
A disagreement about parking
A random argument with no paper trail
To cover a long-running appraisal fraud scheme that Elise finally traced back to her

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: What evidence turns suspicion into proof?

options.label: A rumor thread with no source
A witness who changed stories twice
A drink napkin with no fingerprints
duplicate key impression, staged voicemail metadata, and appraisal fraud binder
Nora Bellamy
Calvin Reed
Brielle St. James
Vivian LaSalle
A random attack outside the building
A fall caused by the crowd
An accidental mix-up at the bar
Used a duplicate Circa 1857 key to move Elise’s emergency medication and frame Calvin with a staged voicemail
Because she won a trivia prize
Because she arrived late
Because she insulted the decor
To cover a long-running appraisal fraud scheme that Elise finally traced back to her

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which location detail is essential?

options.label: The venue has a front door
The bar served cocktails
The antique store had price tags
Heresay Speakeasy is attached to Circa 1857, creating a shared hidden movement path

questions.prompt: Final 5: Which idea best describes the solution?

options.label: The loudest witness was automatically right
Every rumor was equally reliable
The first suspect named by the crowd had to be guilty
The killer used gossip as cover, but the physical and digital evidence exposed the truth
